query: What is the capacity threshold that triggers the large generation interconnection process in NYISO according to the NYISO process manual 23 and guide UG21? Cite the relevant source report and section you got this from
final_answer:
According to the NYISO documents, the capacity threshold that triggers the **Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP)** is **greater than 20 MW** of Generating Facility Capacity.

**Source 1 — NYISO Manual 23 (Transmission Expansion and Interconnection Manual), Version 4.3, Effective Date 02/07/2025:**  
Section 3.1 (Introduction) states:  
> “Large Facilities include Large Generating Facilities (generating facilities that have a Generating Facility Capacity of **more than 20 MW**) and Class Year Transmission Projects. Small Generators are generating facilities **no larger than 20 MW**…”  

Figure 2 in that same section also labels: “Large Facility (larger than 20 MW)” versus “Small Generating Facility (20 MW or less).”

**Source 2 — NYISO OATT Attachment X, Section 30.3.1 (referenced in Manual 23):**  
> “The requirement to submit an Interconnection Request applies to all Large Facilities seeking evaluation under this Attachment X to the ISO OATT, including … **increases in capacity that results in total output in excess of 20 MW** …”  

Attachment X is titled “Standard Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (Applicable to **Generating Facilities that exceed 20 MWs** and to Class Year Transmission Facilities).”

**Source 3 — UG-21 Transmission Expansion and Interconnection User’s Guide, Version 3.0, Effective Date 07/18/2025:**  
While UG-21 primarily describes the newer Standard Interconnection Procedures (SIP), it notes that attestations are required “for Generating Facilities **greater than 20 MW**” and that the Cluster Study Process is applicable to “All Generating Facilities … All facilities **larger than 2 MW** requesting CRIS.” Thus, UG-21 does not itself set the LFIP threshold; it references the tariff and Manual 23 for the 20 MW dividing line between Large and Small Generator procedures.

**Conclusion:** The threshold that triggers the large-generation interconnection process (LFIP) in NYISO is **more than 20 MW** of generating capacity. Facilities at 20 MW or below fall under the Small Generator Interconnection Procedures (SGIP).

The user asks: "What is the capacity threshold that triggers the large generation interconnection process in NYISO according to the NYISO process manual 23 and guide UG21? Cite the relevant source report and section you got this from".

We need to answer: capacity threshold that triggers large generation interconnection process in NYISO. According to NYISO process manual 23 and guide UG21. We need find in sources. Need use web search. Need cite source report and section.

Need locate NYISO process manual 23 (likely OATT Process Manual 23: NYISO Open Access Transmission Tariff? "Process Manual 23" maybe "Attachment Q"? Actually NYISO has "Interconnection Process" for "Large Generation Interconnection". Let's search.

tool_input.query: NYISO process manual 23 large generation interconnection capacity threshold UG21

steps.reasoning: The search results point to NYISO Manual 23 (Transmission Expansion and Interconnection Manual) and UG-21 (Transmission Expansion and Interconnection User's Guide). However, the current version of Manual 23 (v4.3, February 2025) notes that the Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP) and Small Generator Interconnection Procedures (SGIP) have been replaced by the Standard Interconnection Procedures (SIP) in OATT Attachment HH, except for ongoing transition projects. The user specifically asks about "large generation interconnection process" according to "NYISO process manual 23 and guide UG21". Historically, the threshold for LFIP was 20 MW. We need verify exactly from the source report and section. The search snippet didn't include the threshold. Need open the relevant PDF pages, especially Manual 23 section 3.3.1.1 and UG-21 section 6 maybe. We can use get_page_contents to fetch PDF? The tool returns text from cache maybe. Let's try to get contents of the PDF URL.

tool_input.command:
python3 - <<'PY'
import requests, re
url = "https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/2924447/tei_manual.pdf/94a26e65-fd68-98e1-535b-fc41a9536607?t=1722538030491"
r = requests.get(url)
print("status", r.status_code)
print("len", len(r.content))
open("/tmp/nyiso_m23.pdf", "wb").write(r.content)
PY
pdftotext /tmp/nyiso_m23.pdf /tmp/nyiso_m23.txt 2>&1 | head
wc -l /tmp/nyiso_m23.txt

import requests, re

r = requests.get(url)
print("status", r.status_code)
print("len", len(r.content))
open("/work/nyiso_m23.pdf", "wb").write(r.content)
PY
pdftotext /work/nyiso_m23.pdf /work/nyiso_m23.txt
wc -l /work/nyiso_m23.txt
grep -n -i -C 5 "threshold\|subject to\|LFIP\|large facility\|20 MW\|5 MW\|greater than\|more than" /work/nyiso_m23.txt | head -300

python3 - <<'PY'
import requests
url = "https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf/2c727b38-9b4f-1d29-d967-1736d37aca28?t=1722447395298"
r = requests.get(url)
print(r.status_code, len(r.content))

PY
pdftotext /work/nyiso_ug21.pdf /work/nyiso_ug21.txt
wc -l /work/nyiso_ug21.txt

tool_input.query: NYISO OATT Attachment X Section 30.3.1 Large Generating Facility capacity 20 MW threshold

steps.content:
According to the NYISO documents, the capacity threshold that triggers the **Large Facility Interconnection Procedures (LFIP)** is **greater than 20 MW** of Generating Facility Capacity.

**Source 1 — NYISO Manual 23 (Transmission Expansion and Interconnection Manual), Version 4.3, Effective Date 02/07/2025:**
